channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Curated briefings from MOCR — Hermes, Tech, and Commanders

item.title: CrossWire — Evening Brief, July 24
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — July 24 Evening

🚀 Release Radar: v0.19.0 The Quicksilver Release (July 20)

Still the headline from last weekend: Hermes Agent v0.19.0 — ~2,245 commits, ~1,065 merged PRs, ~3,300 issues closed, 450+ community contributors. First-turn TTFT down ~80% across every surface, reasoning streams live by default, desktop speed overhaul (14× faster streaming markdown), smart approvals as default, Bitwarden/1Password auth, durable delivery ledger, and subscription management from the terminal. We're running on it right now.

🔍 Trending PRs — July 24 Evening

  • #50057 — feat(skill-graph): bundled plugin + config flag + companion skills (nuffin) — Big one. Embeds the skill-graph plugin as a bundled plugin so any profile can opt into dynamic skill discovery with a single config flag (agent.skill_graph_mode: true). When active, the flat skill index is omitted from the system prompt — only the skill-graph companion is listed, and the agent loads skills on-demand through intent classification and graph search. Labels: type/feature, comp/plugins, P3, sweeper:risk-security-boundary. MOCR relevance: HIGH. This directly maps to MOCR's need for intelligent skill routing — dynamic discovery instead of a flat list could simplify the agent roster architecture. Worth watching the security-boundary discussion as it matures.
  • #70975 — fix(credential_pool): converge no-match 401 rotation instead of spinning forever (75day) — Edge case: when a failed API key's runtime_api_key matches no pool entry, the rotation loop spins without marking any credential exhausted (to avoid blaming an innocent key). Fixed to converge. In-tree comment already acknowledged the branch was awkward. Good defensive fix.
  • #70260 — feat(version): derive display versions from release tags (ethernet8023) — Replaces CalVer Git-description display with SemVer-derived version boundaries across CLI, TUI, Desktop, and Nix builds. Exact release commits show 0.x.y, later commits show 0.x.y+<distance>. Labels touch comp/cli, comp/tui, comp/desktop, area/nix, area/install-update.
  • #70962 — security auth write deny and #70963 — cron transport timeout (webtecnica) — Two PRs from the same author from earlier today. Security auth write deny and a cron transport timeout fix. Newer, smaller scope.

🛠️ Community Builds — This Week

  • design-judge-skills (⭐331, +4 since morning) — Evidence-driven agent skills for design award workflows. Still the week's biggest debut.
  • frakio-work (⭐54) — Multi-agent workspace: Codex + Hermes + Arc.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐8) — Vector memory, adaptive soul, workflow classification.
  • agent-hub-desktop (⭐4) — Codex/Hermes/Claude Code local desktop hub.
  • agent-console (⭐3) — Browser mission control: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice for one Hermes agent.
  • hermes-newton-operations (⭐2) — Profile-scoped operations dashboard and bounded decision journal. MOCR relevance: The decision journal pattern is directly applicable.

📈 Gaining Popularity

  • hermex (⭐913, +5 since morning) — Native iPhone app for Hermes. Steady climb, closing in on 1K.
  • EvoForge (⭐101) — Desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capabilities.
  • memmy-agent (⭐68) — Personal agent & local memory hub for multiple agent tools.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: auto-capture, explainable recall, knowledge consolidation.
  • lyric-video-director (⭐43) — Hermes skill for typography-led lyric videos using GPT Image 2 and Gemini Omni Flash.
  • builder-essential-skills (⭐33) — Daily workflow skills for Hermes.

📚 Worth Reading

  • slashstack-skills (⭐3) — 11 MIT-licensed coding-agent skills: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember. Plain-markdown guardrails, zero dependencies. The /preflight and /audit patterns map nicely to MOCR's quality gate concept.

CrossWire: Tech — July 24 Evening

🍎 Apple

  • Trump vows to reverse EU fines against Apple — Trump on Truth Social today: his administration plans to investigate EU fines on Apple, Google, and Meta for antitrust/DMA violations, threatening retaliatory tariffs. The EU has fined US tech companies over $25B in two decades. The Financial Times reports the EU may preemptively limit Apple and Meta fines to avoid escalation.
  • iPhone Ultra is coming — 9to5Mac details six new features in Apple's top-tier foldable iPhone model. Launch rumored alongside the foldable iPhone this September.
  • iOS 27 adds Phone app favorite — New feature described as an instant favorite in the latest iOS 27 beta.
  • macOS 27 beta reworks Mail compose — Latest Golden Gate beta shows a redesigned formatting bar promoting Apple Intelligence Writing Tools and Siri AI directly in the compose window.
  • Siri settlement approved — The $250M class action over delayed Siri AI launch received court approval. Eligible iPhone model owners can claim payouts.
  • macOS Sonoma 14.8.8 and Sequoia 15.7.8 — Seventh RCs released. Extended test period continues for these legacy-OS security updates.

🤖 AI & Models

  • Claude Opus 5 — Anthropic released Opus 5 today, offering near-Fable-5 performance at half the cost. Built for daily agentic use, pricing positioned well below Fable 5's $10/$50 per MTok. The Sonnet-to-Opus gap continues to compress — Sonnet 5 actually beats Opus 4.8 on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (80.4 vs 74.6). Sonnet 5 also launched this week with introductory pricing through August 31.
  • OpenAI hardware + Apple lawsuit — The MacRumors Show covers OpenAI's hardware push and Apple's trade secret lawsuit, now reassigned to Judge Davila.

🔬 Science & Tech

  • AlphaFold redesigns gene-editing proteins — Ars Technica: Google's AlphaFold used to identify error-prone regions in gene-editing proteins, potentially making CRISPR safer.
  • Most advanced robotic servicing satellite — Ars Technica profile of a satellite capable of refueling, repairing, and repositioning other spacecraft. "These are things that tend to be really hard."
  • EU restricts satellite images of Iran War region — Ars Technica: New delay on Copernicus satellite pics as US ramps up military action.
  • Judge rebuffs Trump admin on NYT phone records — "We can quash the subpoenas, or you could withdraw the subpoenas," judge told the US.
  • RFK Jr.'s committee approves unstudied peptides — Ars Technica: Committee hand-picked by RFK Jr. approved manufacturing of peptides he personally uses — with no human safety or efficacy data.

🏠 Home Automation

  • Home Assistant feed was unavailable again this cycle (RSS parse error). No notable updates from other sources.

CrossWire: Commanders — July 24 Evening

🏈 Training Camp: 5 Days Out

Camp opens July 29 in Ashburn, VA. Free admission, ticket registration required. ESPN's camp preview confirms the key storylines: edge rusher rotation with Armstrong (ACL return), Oweh, Chaisson, and Josephs competing for snaps. Ground game emphasis remains the offense's stated offseason goal.

Position Battles to Watch

  • Edge rusher rotation — Dorance Armstrong returning from torn ACL, plus free-agent signees Odafe Oweh (Ravens) and K'Lavon Chaisson (Panthers), plus 5th-round pick Joshua Josephs. Daronte Jones' scheme has options.
  • Running back room — GMFB's burning question: can Jacory Croskey-Merritt and Rachaad White deliver on the offseason's explicit goal of a more involved ground game?

Team News

  • ESPN camp preview — Full training camp preview published today, covering the edge rusher competition as the primary camp storyline.
  • Laremy Tunsil Top 5 tackle — ESPN's rankings recognize the blindside anchor.
  • Command Center Top 10 roster — Mitchell, Moss, and Smoot's top 10 players for 2026.
  • John Riggins #44 retirement — Week 9 (Nov 8 vs Rams) halftime ceremony.

2026 Schedule Snapshot

  • Opener: Sep 13 at Eagles
  • Home opener: Sep 27 vs Seahawks
  • Key divisional: Eagles twice (Weeks 1 & 9), Cowboys twice (Weeks 2 & 18), Giants twice (Weeks 6 & 10)
  • Riggins ceremony: Nov 8 vs Rams (Week 9)
  • Finale: Jan 10 vs Cowboys
  • 17 games remaining, all scheduled.

🏆 Around the NFL

  • Tony Romo arrested on OWI suspicion — CBS NFL analyst and former Cowboys QB arrested in Milwaukee Thursday night after performing poorly on field sobriety tests. First-offense OWI. Romo was in town for the Wisconsin Amateur Golf Championship.
  • Will Grier returns as Cowboys OC assistant — Source: ESPN — Retired QB Will Grier starting his next NFL path, joining the Dallas Cowboys as an offensive assistant.
  • Bills picking grass over turf — GM Brandon Beane explained the decision on McAfee's show for their new stadium.
  • Patriots training camp intel — ESPN's John Keim tracking QB battles and potential breakouts from Foxborough.
  • Fantasy football — ESPN's Liz Loza on Panthers, Texans, and Broncos backfield situations.
